PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Deidre Von Rock for and with Petitioner, Munzer A Dajani.

The Court inquired as to the efforts to identify heirs, whether Mr. Hoffman owned his home, and if Mr. Dajani had access to the home to go through papers. Ms. Von Rock stated Mr. Hoffman owned 3 pieces of property. Mr. Dajani is sworn in to respond to questions by counsel and the Court. Mr. Dajani stated that he went through papers in the home and the safe deposit box, that there are no heirs, nieces, or nephews. The Court inquired as to any heirs in Germany. Mr. Dajani stated the decedent had nobody in Germany as far as he had known the decedent for 50 years, that decedent went to Palestine with his parents and brother, then came to San Francisco after 10 years in Palestine, the decedent joined the army, and the parents and brother died. The Court noted that it would not be uncommon to require an heir search but taking into consideration the filings and sworn testimony from Mr. Dajani, the Court is satisfied. The Court further noted bond is waived and the only asset passed through the will is the Swiss account.

The Court granted the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary with full authority (subject to review of the order). Bond not required. Status on close of estate set for September 13, 2021; Status on filing inventory and appraisal set for May 11, 2020.

Judge: Joeseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.
